summary: stdin-only CLI refactor mission delivers fully on contract: 10 write subcommands migrated to readInputData() 0-arg, 20 JSON-input docs sites rewritten to stdin heredoc/pipe/redirection, plugin/bin/geas rebundled and end-to-end smoke verified (pipe/heredoc/redirection + TTY hang-guard). 31/31 tests green across 3 commits (ad3698d, 185ebe5, 44c12c4). Repo-wide grep shows 0 JSON-input --data/--file violations; allowed value-flag exceptions (event log / context write / packet create) preserved. 6 debt items recorded (0 critical, 0 high, 2 normal: DEBT-001 real-Windows shell smoke, DEBT-005 helper extraction; 4 low). No critical/high security findings.
rationale: All three tasks passed their gates with independent specialist approvals (software-engineer, design-authority, qa-engineer, technical-writer, platform-engineer, security-engineer). Acceptance criteria from spec are fully met: (1) --data/--file hard-removed from all 10 write subcommands, (2) TTY guard emits NO_STDIN with heredoc/pipe/redirection examples and exits non-zero without hanging, (3) all three stdin patterns work in smoke tests, (4) repo-wide grep returns 0 non-whitelisted hits, (5) bundle regenerated via npm run build && npm run bundle, (6) 31/31 tests pass. The product problem is solved end-to-end: AI agents calling geas via Bash tool no longer have two confusing input paths, the stale --data examples that were causing copy-paste failures are gone, and the bundled binary that agents actually execute now reflects the refactor. Debt is honestly booked and appropriately sized — DEBT-001 (real-Windows shell matrix) and DEBT-002 (POSIX shebang CI smoke) are verification gaps that mock coverage makes acceptable for ship, and DEBT-003/004/005/006 are structural polish worth a follow-up refactor mission but not blocking. Korean docs sync was correctly carved out as a separate mission per non-goals — do not expand scope retroactively. The gap-assessment recommendation of a shell-matrix follow-up and a helper-extraction refactor is sound and should be picked up next. Ship.
notes: Recommended follow-ups: (1) shell live-smoke matrix mission for DEBT-001 + DEBT-002, (2) helper extraction refactor for DEBT-004/005/006, (3) protocol enhancement — task-compiler should verify CLI subcommand existence before compiling contracts (task-001 retrospective surfaced a phantom 'evolution proposal' subcommand).
